title: "@Cache*" annotations should only be applied on concrete classes
description: Applying Spring's @Cacheable, @CachePut, or @CacheEvict annotations to abstract classes or interfaces can lead to unexpected behavior, as caching proxies cannot be created for non-concrete types. This rule ensures that caching annotations are only used on concrete classes to guarantee correct proxying and caching behavior.
recommendation: Apply @Cache* annotations only to concrete classes. Refactor any abstract classes or interfaces with these annotations so that only concrete implementations are annotated.
impact: Incorrect use of caching annotations on abstract classes or interfaces may result in caching not being applied as expected, leading to performance issues or incorrect application behavior.

checks.message: @Cache* annotation used on abstract class or interface.
